what_it_does: This file is a structured design-system extraction and pattern inventory for a 17-slide ConceptV investor pitch deck, cataloging slide-level attention flow, reusable visual patterns, components, content-slot budgets, and narrative grammar.
observations: The file documents slides 1–17 of a longer 49-page deck, describing each slide's content zones and intended eye path.

It contains 40+ findings grouped by category, including gradient/shading, geometry, type hierarchy, padding/alignment, color palette, atoms, line connectors, texture, chrome mode, zones, and sequence.
Each finding includes a recipe, slide evidence, an emergent flag, and a prescriptive generator rule.
It defines nine reusable components such as StatCell, FlowDiagram, NetworkGraph, NodeChain, ProposalCard, and BarChartCell.
It provides content_samples with character, word, and line budgets for slots like H1 titles, section heads, body paragraphs, bullets, stats, and credentials.
It records precise design tokens: colors (#F8F5 background, #C8A020 gold, #1E1E1A text), margins (44px/36px), card padding (18px), and type sizes.
It identifies a single light/warm chrome mode with no dark variant.
It uses strict shape semantics: hexagon for the system/Property Wizard, octagon for Virtual Tours product node, rounded rectangles for process steps, and circles for stakeholders.
It notes a circled-V logomark bug appears on every non-cover slide.
It distinguishes gold dashed lines for process/projection from solid grey lines for static grouping.
standouts: The slide titles across p02–p17 are authored as clauses of one continuous spoken-pitch sentence connected by leading words like By, With, Which, But, To, That, And.
The same flow diagram appears first as a secondary visual on p04 and is later promoted to the primary visual on p09 without modification.
A faint global blueprint/topographic watermark texture at roughly 5–7% opacity unifies all content slides.
Shape meaning is tightly controlled: hexagon = system, octagon = product output, rounded rectangle = process step, circle = network node.
conventions: Consistent schema for findings: category, name, recipe, evidence, emergent flag, generator_rule.
Every pattern is paired with a prescriptive generator rule rather than a neutral description alone.
Components include a reuse_note linking them to specific slides.
Content samples include measurable budgets for length and line count.
Findings are tagged as emergent or non-emergent to separate observed patterns from intended ones.
Language stays descriptive and avoids ranking or evaluative comparisons.
concerns: The depth_level field says the file is part 1 of 3 of a 49-page deck, but only slides 1–17 are covered; the scope gap is not explained.
Some color and spacing values are approximate ('or similar'), which limits precision for automated generation.
Several generator rules are phrased as absolute prohibitions ('never add a veil', 'non-negotiable') without noting trade-offs.
Evidence for global background texture lists all 16 slides explicitly, which is verbose and could be represented as a cross-cutting token.
The file has no code symbols, so the schema's symbols array is empty even though the file itself is a substantive design artifact.
notes: A dense, tokenized design grammar for a pitch deck that treats layout, typography, shape semantics, and narrative sequence as reproducible rules.
questions: What source files (PDF, Figma, images) were used to derive these measurements and rules?
How do the 1-page and 15-page versions referenced in depth_level differ in applying these patterns?
What tooling is intended to consume this substrate for slide generation or design audit?
Why does this extraction cover only slides 1–17 if it is described as part 1 of 3 of a 49-page deck?
Are the color/spacing values measured from raster images or taken from an existing style guide?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is a long-form, low-compression design grammar source for the pitch deck, exactly as its metadata describes.
apparent_themes: Systematic visual grammar for investor storytelling
Semiotic rigor in shapes and colors
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to encode the pitch deck's visual and rhetorical design as a machine-readable generative specification so the deck can be reproduced, extended, or audited against a shared grammar.
novelty: The treatment of slide titles as a single continuous grammatical sentence and the promotion of the same diagram from supporting to primary register across slides are distinctive, as are the detailed attention-flow descriptions and content-slot budgets.
contribution: This file contributes a granular, tokenized design vocabulary (colors, spacing, shapes, typography, components) plus narrative and attention-flow rules that a fused system could keep when generating or validating pitch-deck layouts.
summary_for_embedding: A structured design-system extraction of the ConceptV investor pitch deck (slides 1–17) documenting narrative arc, per-slide attention flow, reusable visual patterns, components, content-slot budgets, and generator rules.
intention_for_embedding: To capture the pitch deck's visual grammar and rhetorical sequence as a reproducible, machine-readable substrate for design generation or consistency review.
